headline: FELDA Settlers Owe RM8.3B in Unrecoverable Loans
context: FELDA's 2025 annual report revealed that 112,000 settlers collectively owe RM8.3 billion in loans, with 58% of balances classified as non-performing after decades of low CPO prices and mismanaged restructuring programmes.

cards.big: 112,000 FELDA settlers owe RM8.3 billion — average debt of RM74,000 per family with no means to repay
cards.sub: Many debts originated from 1980s replanting loans that ballooned with compound interest over 40 years

cards.big: FELDA wrote off RM4.2 billion in settler debts in 2019 but new loans of RM3.8 billion accumulated by 2025
cards.sub: Debt cycle continues because settler income at RM1,600 monthly cannot cover both living costs and repayment

cards.big: Second-generation FELDA youth — 180,000 families — have no legal land title despite parents' decades of labour
cards.sub: Land ownership limbo prevents mortgage access and wealth transfer that other rural communities enjoy

cards.big: FELDA board spent RM6.3 billion on failed investments including London hotel and Eagle High plantation before 2018
cards.sub: Settler debt persists while those who approved disastrous investments faced no personal financial consequences

cards.big: FELDA settlers cleared the jungle and built Malaysia's palm oil wealth — now they retire in debt while the wealth went elsewhere
Should all FELDA settler debts older than 20 years be written off and replaced with unconditional land titles?
